description: Web tool that analyzes ML paper methods sections and outputs reproducibility scores, gap reports, SHAP-based evidence highlights, and fix hints

userStories.title: Data Pipeline Setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to create the data pipeline to download, process, and split the Papers With Code dataset for model training.
acceptanceCriteria: Download Papers With Code reproducibility annotations (papers-with-abstracts.json.gz)
Fetch PDFs from arXiv API for each paper
Extract methods section from PDFs using regex-based section detection
Clean text (remove LaTeX, URLs, normalize whitespace)
Stratified split: 75% train, 12.5% val, 12.5% test
Save as Parquet files in data/processed/
Log failed extractions to failed_extractions.txt
userStories.notes: Week 1 deliverable. Expected ~1200 papers, ~900 train rows.

userStories.title: PDF Extractor Module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a module to extract the methods/methodology section from ML paper PDFs.
acceptanceCriteria: Use PyMuPDF (fitz) to extract full text preserving paragraph structure
Detect methods section using regex patterns for section headers
Find section boundaries using END_MARKERS (results, experiments, conclusion, etc.)
Fallback to first 2000 chars if no methods section found
Support extraction from URL for live API input
Max output: 2000 tokens (truncate if longer)
userStories.notes: Week 1 deliverable. Critical for both training data and live inference.

userStories.title: Baseline Classifier (TF-IDF + LR)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a baseline model to establish the performance floor before fine-tuning SciBERT.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement TF-IDF vectorization + Logistic Regression
Train on methods section text
Evaluate AUROC, F1, precision, recall on test set
Expected baseline AUROC: 0.65-0.72
Document results in notebook 02_baseline_experiments.ipynb
userStories.notes: Week 2 deliverable. This is the 'floor' that SciBERT must beat.

userStories.title: SciBERT Classifier Module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to fine-tune SciBERT for binary reproducibility classification.
acceptanceCriteria: Load allenai/scibert_scivocab_uncased from HuggingFace
Fine-tune with HuggingFace Trainer API (5 epochs, lr=2e-5)
Handle long texts with sliding window (512 tokens, 64 overlap)
Return score (0-1), label, confidence, logits
Log training to Weights & Biases
Target AUROC > 0.78 (must beat baseline by >5 points)
Save checkpoint to models/scibert_finetuned/

userStories.title: NeurIPS Checklist JSON
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to create the checklist JSON with 43 items for gap detection.
acceptanceCriteria: Transcribe all 43 NeurIPS reproducibility checklist items
Format: id, category, item, severity, keywords
Severity levels: high (seeds, hyperparams), medium (hardware), low (limitations)
Save to data/checklist/neurips_checklist.json

userStories.title: Gap Detector Module
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see which reproducibility checklist items are missing from a paper.
acceptanceCriteria: Load sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2 model
Split methods text into sentences with nltk.sent_tokenize()
Compute cosine similarity between sentences and checklist items
Flag items as MISSING if max_similarity < 0.35 threshold
Return status, similarity_score, best_matching_sentence per item
Generate summary with coverage_score and missing counts by severity
Target precision > 0.68 on manual eval (30 papers)

userStories.title: SHAP Explainability Module
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see which sentences drove the reproducibility score.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement ablation-based SHAP approximation for sentence attribution
Compute SHAP value per sentence by masking and comparing predictions
Normalize scores to [-1, 1] range
Return top 5 most influential sentences with rank and normalized_score
Generate highlighted text with color coding (green/yellow/none)
Cache results by text hash to avoid re-computation
Performance: ~6s for 20-sentence text on CPU

userStories.title: Fix Hint Generator Module
userStories.description: As a user, I want actionable suggestions to fix each detected gap.
acceptanceCriteria: Call Mistral-7B-Instruct via HuggingFace Inference API (free tier)
Generate one-line fix hints per missing checklist item
Use structured prompt with gap item, severity, context
Graceful degradation if no API token (skip hints, tool still works)
Target cost: $0 (free tier), <2s per hint
userStories.notes: Week 4 deliverable. Turns detection into actionable repair.

userStories.title: FastAPI Backend
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a REST API to expose the ML pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /analyze endpoint accepting PDF upload or arxiv URL
GET /health endpoint for status checks
Pydantic models for request/response validation
Return: score, label, confidence, gaps, explanations, hints
Response time < 30s for full pipeline
CORS enabled for frontend

userStories.title: Streamlit Frontend
userStories.description: As a user, I want a web interface to upload papers and see results.
acceptanceCriteria: File upload widget for PDF or URL input
Display reproducibility score with gauge visualization
Show gap report table sorted by severity
Highlight methods text with SHAP-based sentence coloring
Display fix hints per missing item
Responsive design, works on mobile
Loading spinner during analysis

userStories.title: HuggingFace Spaces Deployment
userStories.description: As a user, I want to access the tool online without local setup.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Dockerfile for HF Spaces deployment
Deploy Streamlit app to HuggingFace Spaces (free tier)
App loads in < 60s cold start
Public URL accessible without login
Add README with demo screenshots

userStories.title: Documentation and Publication Draft
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want comprehensive documentation and a paper draft.
acceptanceCriteria: Complete README with installation, usage, architecture diagram
API documentation for all endpoints
Write 4-page paper draft: intro, method, results, conclusion
Include baseline comparison table, SHAP examples, gap detection accuracy
Resume bullet: quantified metrics, tech stack, live URL
